svg.text: Otto drives the Claude Code CLI against a target repo while you’re AFK. Each iteration renders prompt templates, then
runs a gate stage (implement / verify / apply-review) → reviewer directly on the host, accumulating a git-tracked memory.
The loop exits early when the gate returns <promise>NO MORE TASKS</promise>.
Shims (afk.sh / ghafk.sh) → bins (apps/cli, no build) → @phamvuhoang/otto-core runAfk / runGhAfk → runBin
Prompt assembly before every stage (renderTemplate in render.ts) — expanded in this order
issues.json for ghafk); only the path enters.
Host shell via execSync. !? returns fallback on
failure; plain ! throws and aborts the iteration.
passed on the otto-afk command line.
runStage / streamClaude: write .run prompt → spawn claude on host → parse stream-json NDJSON · result event → { result, total_cost_usd, is_error }
Shell, git, gh, repo filesystem on the host.
natively from the host. Same-shell rule: run
otto from the shell where you logged in.
for every claude invocation — the loop’s entire
blast radius. Must be a git repo.
Otto Loop Runtime (runBin → runLoop → executeStage → runStage / streamClaude)
Run modes — gate stage (one runs per loop, by bin + flags)
Injected into every gate & reviewer prompt; agents append durable conventions, gotchas & decisions as they work — so each iteration starts smarter. Rides in the work commit.
(per-run, host-side — <workspace>/.otto-tmp/, gitignored — plus durable .otto/ state and the guardrails that bound the blast radius)
finally; may leak on SIGKILL (safe to delete).
event stream per stage, kept for post-mortems.
Live console rendering of stream-json events.
Default sandbox runner (Seatbelt) confines writes to the workspace (git-recoverable); bypassPermissions
scoped to workspace — OTTO_RUNNER=host runs unsandboxed (git-recoverable workspace only).
(cross-cutting — how Otto itself ships; release-please automates npm versions)
Each iteration loops Gate → Implement → Review with freshly rendered prompts and a growing .otto/LEARNINGS.md memory, running
claude directly on the host — the sentinel <promise>NO MORE TASKS</promise> exits early, otherwise the iteration budget runs out.
